name: pip2pi
perf_notes: Build-time CLI, not a server: pip2tgz throughput is bounded entirely by pip's download speed and dependency resolution; dir2pi is a single linear pass over the package directory that emits per-package index.html files and (by default) symlinks the artifacts into simple/, so it is fast and disk-cheap. No published benchmarks, no latency/memory targets - runtime serving performance is whatever HTTP server or CDN fronts the generated static tree.
scale_notes: No HA, clustering, or multi-node features in the tool itself; it is a one-shot index generator run from a single process. Each run is a full re-build of the simple/ tree (no incremental state, no database). The static output is trivially fronted by any scalable web server/CDN, but that is a property of the chosen server, not pip2pi. Remote distribution is handled by shelling out to rsync over SSH when TARGET is a host:path.
summary: A command-line tool that downloads packages with their dependencies and turns a directory of sdists/wheels into a static PEP 503 "simple" index you serve with any plain web server (or rsync to a remote host).
extras: pip2tgz mirrors a package PLUS all transitive dependencies by delegating to `pip download`/`pip install -d`, accepting any pip-recognized spec (pins, URLs, local paths, requirements.txt)

dir2pi builds the simple/ index using symlinks into the package directory by default (--symlink/--no-symlink) so artifacts aren't duplicated, and recognizes .egg, .whl and source archives via file_to_package()
pip2pi wrapper can deploy to a remote host via rsync when TARGET contains a colon (host:path)
PEP 503 name normalization is configurable: --normalize-package-names (default), --no-normalize-package-names, and --aggressive-normalization for pip 6/7 compatibility
Index is usable fully offline via file:// URLs as well as over HTTP
--no-build-html to skip HTML generation and --also-get-source to fetch sdists alongside wheels
